case_title: MARLON DULAY VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: REPLY TO OPPOS. TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE; POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY PETITIONER DULAY, MARLON
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY PETITIONER DULAY, MARLON
ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER MARLON DULAY'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. RESPONDENT MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PETITIONER'S BAC EXCEEDED .08% UNDER PETRICKA V. DMV (2001) 89 CAL.APP.4TH 1341 AND BAKER V. GOURLEY (2000) 81 CAL.APP.4TH 1167, 1172-1173. THE PRESUMPTION OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 664 APPLIES AND SATISFIED THE DMV'S INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF IN THIS CASE. THE OFFICER IN COMPLIANCE WITH AN OFFICIAL DUTY PROVIDED HIS SWORN REPORT ON FORM DS 367 WITH THE RESULTS OF THE TEST AS REQUIRED BY VEHICLE CODE SECTIONS 13380 AND 23612. THE BURDEN THEN SHIFTED TO PETITIONER TO SHOW THAT THE OFFICER DID NOT CARRY OUT HIS OFFICIAL DUTIES. PETITIONER SHOWED THAT OFFICER WERLE REPORTED THE TESTS TO THE THIRD DECIMAL PLACE IN VIOLATION OF 17 CCR 1220.4 AND THAT HE SIGNED THE WRONG BOX ON THE FORM. THOSE ERRORS WERE DE MINIMUS AND ARE NOT THE TYPE OF AFFIRMATIVE EVIDENCE NECESSARY TO SHOW THAT OFFICER WERELE FAILED TO OBSERVE THE OFFICIAL STANDARDS. THE HEARING OFFICER DID NOT ERR IN FINDING MR. ZEHNDER'S TESTIMONY SPECULATIVE AND PETITIONER FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE ADMINISTRATIVE FINDINGS ARE CONTRARY TO THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Alt Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER MARLON DULAY'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is denied. Respondent maintained its initial burden of showing that Petitioner's BAC exceeded .08% under Petricka v. DMV (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 1341 and Baker v. Gourley (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 1167, 1172-1173. The presumption of Evidence Code section 664 applies and satisfied the DMV's initial burden of proof in this case. The officer in compliance with an official duty provided his sworn report on form DS 367 with the results of the test as required by Vehicle Code sections 13380 and 23612. The burden then shifted to Petitioner to show that the officer did not carry out his official duties. Petitioner showed that Officer Werle reported the tests to the third decimal place in violation of 17 CCR 1220.4 and that he signed the wrong box on the form. Those errors were de minimus and are not the type of affirmative evidence necessary to show that Officer Werele failed to observe the official standards. The hearing officer did not err in finding Mr. Zehnder's testimony speculative and Petitioner failed to show that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Alt Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Continued To Jan-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification. PETITIONER MARLON DULAY'S Petition For Alt Writ Of Mandate is Continued to January 30, 2014 to give Petitioner the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of the petition and all moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 20, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the matter will be taken off calendar. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition, reply and administrative record. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185
